config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi agar tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas kelompok kecil atau suara yang berbeda. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat boleh menuntut tanggung jawab, tetapi hak dasar individu tetap harus jadi pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the greater good” can be used to excuse abuse of power. Public safety and social order matter, but they have to be limited by strong individual rights, or minorities and unpopular people get crushed. At the same time, I do not think individual rights mean doing whatever you want with no responsibility; rights and duties have to be balanced. So for me, society can set rules, but those rules should protect people first, not treat them as disposable for the collective.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahwa ketika “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai terlalu longgar, itu sering jadi alasan untuk membungkam kritik, mengorbankan minoritas, atau mengabaikan hak warga kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dijalankan lewat batas yang jelas: hak dasar, perlindungan hukum, dan akuntabilitas negara tidak boleh dikorbankan. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan antara tanggung jawab sosial dan martabat tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have blanket priority over the individual, because in the United States that phrase has too often been used to justify overreach and silence dissent. At the same time, I do not buy the idea that individual rights exist in a vacuum; rights come with obligations, and things like public health, public safety, and basic laws are legitimate limits. What I reject is the claim that the collective should automatically win, since that makes it too easy to sacrifice minorities, unpopular speech, or personal freedom for convenience. For me, the right standard is that society can demand responsibility, but it must never treat individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Indonesia justru sering penyalahgunaan muncul saat dalih “kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu luas. Saya paham ada kebutuhan untuk aturan bersama, seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi ketat supaya tidak berubah jadi alat menekan kritik atau minoritas. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling keras menuntut kepatuhan, melainkan yang berani melindungi hak dasar bahkan ketika itu tidak nyaman bagi mayoritas. Jadi, prioritasnya harus keseimbangan yang tegas, bukan pengorbanan individu demi kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general principle, because in the United States that idea has historically been a convenient excuse for government overreach and for ignoring unpopular minorities. Public health, safety, and basic order are real needs, but they only stay legitimate when they are tightly limited and subject to strong rights protections. What I reject is the claim that the collective should automatically win, because that turns people into tools for convenience instead of citizens with dignity. A healthy society protects individual rights first and then asks people to carry their responsibilities within those limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
